Total Participants - N.evidence: In the 'RESULTS' section, under the 'PATIENTS' subsection, it states: 'From July 2006 through December 2012, a total of 790 patients were enrolled and underwent randomization.'

Control Arm - N.evidence: The 'RESULTS' section states 'A total of 790 patients (median age, 63 years) underwent randomization.' Table 1, 'Baseline Characteristics of the Patients,' lists 'ADT Alone (N=393)' as one of the groups, which represents the control arm.

Number of Arms Included.evidence: The 'METHODS' section states: 'We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel... or ADT alone.' This indicates two treatment arms.

Treatment Arm(s).evidence: The 'METHODS' section states: 'We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel (at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area every 3 weeks for six cycles) or ADT alone.' The 'TREATMENT PLAN, STRATIFICATION, AND RANDOMIZATION' section further clarifies: 'Patients were randomly assigned to ADT alone or to combination therapy with ADT plus docetaxel at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area given every 3 weeks for six cycles'. This indicates 'ADT plus docetaxel' is the experimental treatment arm.

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: In the 'RESULTS' section, under 'PATIENTS', it states that 'A total of 790 patients... underwent randomization'. Table 1, 'Baseline Characteristics of the Patients', further breaks down the patient numbers for each arm, showing 'ADT plus Docetaxel (N=397)' as the experimental arm.

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: The value '28.9 months' is explicitly stated in the 'RESULTS' section, first paragraph, as 'After a median follow-up of 28.9 months'. It is also reiterated in the 'PATIENTS' subsection of 'RESULTS' and in the caption for Figure 1, 'The median duration of follow-up was 28.9 months among all patients'.

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: ADT plus docetaxel (at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area every 3 weeks for six cycles), with premedication with 8 mg of oral dexamethasone at 12 hours, 3 hours, and 1 hour before docetaxel infusion.
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: The 'METHODS' section states: 'We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel (at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area every 3 weeks for six cycles) or ADT alone.' The 'TREATMENT PLAN, STRATIFICATION, AND RANDOMIZATION' section further details the combination therapy: 'Patients were randomly assigned to ADT alone or to combination therapy with ADT plus docetaxel at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area given every 3 weeks for six cycles, with premedication with 8 mg of oral dexamethasone at 12 hours, 3 hours, and 1 hour before docetaxel infusion.' This describes the specific regimen for Treatment Arm 1 (the combination therapy arm).

Control Arm.evidence: The 'ABSTRACT' section states: 'We assessed whether concomitant treatment with ADT plus docetaxel would result in longer overall survival than that with ADT alone.' This indicates 'ADT alone' as the comparison group. The 'METHODS' section further confirms: 'We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel ... or ADT alone.' Table 1 also lists 'ADT Alone (N=393)' as one of the study arms.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: The 'METHODS' section states: 'We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel... or ADT alone.' Treatment Arm 1 is 'ADT plus docetaxel'. ADT (Androgen-deprivation therapy) is a hormonal therapy, and docetaxel is a chemotherapy agent. The article title also uses 'Chemohormonal Therapy' to describe this combination.
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Quality of Life Scale.evidence: The document focuses on clinical outcomes such as overall survival, time to progression, PSA levels, and adverse events. There is no mention of any 'Quality of Life Scale' or similar assessment of participants' overall well-being or satisfaction with their health status.

Quality of Life reported.evidence: The document focuses on clinical outcomes such as overall survival, time to progression, PSA levels, and adverse events. There is no mention of 'Quality of Life' or any related patient-reported outcome measures being assessed or reported in the abstract, methods, results, or discussion sections.

Trial.evidence: The study is explicitly referred to as 'the E3805 study' in the 'METHODS' section, specifically in the paragraph starting 'In this study, the E3805 study, patients received ADT alone or ADT plus docetaxel...' and again under 'STUDY OVERSIGHT'.

Median Age (years) | Treatment.evidence: The 'RESULTS' section on page 740 states: 'The median age was 64 years (range, 36 to 88) in the combination group'. This is further confirmed in Table 1, under 'Age—yr', where the 'Median' for 'ADT plus Docetaxel (N=397)' is listed as '64'.

Median Age (years) | Control.evidence: The 'RESULTS' section on page 740 states: 'The median age was ... 63 years (range, 39 to 91) in the ADT-alone group'. This is further confirmed in Table 1, under 'Age—yr', where the 'Median' for 'ADT Alone (N=393)' is listed as '63'.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.evidence: The document specifies that docetaxel was given for six cycles in the treatment arm, but it does not provide a median on-treatment duration in months for the entire combination therapy (ADT plus docetaxel).

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Cont.evidence: The document mentions that the control arm received ADT alone, but it does not provide a median on-treatment duration in months for this therapy.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treat.evidence: Found in Table 1, 'Baseline Characteristics of the Patients', under the 'ADT plus Docetaxel (N=397)' column, for 'Volume of metastases - High'.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Contr.evidence: Found in Table 1, 'Baseline Characteristics of the Patients', under the 'ADT Alone (N=393)' column, for 'Volume of metastases - High'.

Volume of disease - N (%) | Low | Contro.evidence: Found in Table 1, 'Baseline Characteristics of the Patients', under the 'ADT Alone (N=393)' column, for 'Volume of metastases - Low'.

NCT.evidence: Found in the 'CONCLUSIONS' section: '(Funded by the National Cancer Institute and others; ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT00309985.)'

PubMed ID.evidence: A PubMed ID was not explicitly reported in the document. A DOI (10.1056/NEJMoa1503747) was found, but not a PubMed ID.

Trial Name.value: Chemohormonal Therapy in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer
Trial Name.evidence: Extracted from the main title of the article on the first page.

Author.evidence: Extracted from the author list on the first page: 'Christopher J. Sweeney, M.B., B.S., Yu-Hui Chen, M.S., M.P.H., Michael Carducci, M.D., Glenn Liu, M.D., David F. Jarrard, M.D., Mario Eisenberger, M.D., Yu-Ning Wong, M.D., M.S.C.E., Noah Hahn, M.D., Manish Kohli, M.D., Matthew M. Cooney, M.D., Robert Dreicer, M.D., Nicholas J. Vogelzang, M.D., Joel Picus, M.D., Daniel Shevrin, M.D., Maha Hussain, M.B., Ch.B., Jorge A. Garcia, M.D., and Robert S. DiPaola, M.D.'

Year.evidence: Found in the publication details on the first page: 'N Engl J Med 2015;373:737-46.'

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document is labeled 'ORIGINAL ARTICLE' and contains extensive sections including BACKGROUND, METHODS, RESULTS, and DISCUSSION, indicating it is a full publication rather than just an abstract.

Phase.evidence: In the 'DISCUSSION' section, the text states: 'the definition was reproducible across two phase 3 studies conducted in a similar time period.'

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The document is explicitly labeled 'ORIGINAL ARTICLE' on the first page.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: The document describes the study population as men with 'metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer' at the time of enrollment, implying synchronous disease. However, it does not explicitly report results by a prognostic group specifically defined as 'synchronous' versus 'metachronous' metastases. The stratification was based on 'high or low burden of metastatic disease', not the timing of metastasis onset.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: The document describes the study population as men with 'metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer' at the time of enrollment. It does not mention or report results by a prognostic group specifically defined as 'metachronous' metastases. The stratification was based on 'high or low burden of metastatic disease', not the timing of metastasis onset.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: On page 3, under 'TREATMENT PLAN, STRATIFICATION, AND RANDOMIZATION', it states: 'Patients were also stratified according to... the extent of metastases (high volume [defined as the presence of visceral metastases or ≥4 bone lesions with ≥1 beyond the vertebral bodies and pelvis] vs. low volume)'. Figure 1B is titled 'Patients with High-Volume Disease' and presents survival data for this subgroup, indicating reporting by this prognostic group.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: On page 3, under 'TREATMENT PLAN, STRATIFICATION, AND RANDOMIZATION', it states: 'Patients were also stratified according to... the extent of metastases (high volume [defined as the presence of visceral metastases or ≥4 bone lesions with ≥1 beyond the vertebral bodies and pelvis] vs. low volume)'. Figure 1C is titled 'Patients with Low-Volume Disease' and presents survival data for this subgroup, indicating reporting by this prognostic group.

PS - N (%) | 0 | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 1, under 'ECOG performance status — no. (%)‡', row '0', column 'ADT plus Docetaxel (N=397)'.

PS - N (%) | 0 | Control.evidence: Found in Table 1, under 'ECOG performance status — no. (%)‡', row '0', column 'ADT Alone (N=393)'.

PS - N (%) | 1-2 | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 1, under 'ECOG performance status — no. (%)‡'. For Treatment (ADT plus Docetaxel, N=397), PS 1 is 114 (28.7%) and PS 2 is 6 (1.5%). Summing these gives 114+6 = 120 patients, and 28.7%+1.5% = 30.2%.

PS - N (%) | 1-2 | Control.evidence: Found in Table 1, under 'ECOG performance status — no. (%)‡'. For Control (ADT Alone, N=393), PS 1 is 115 (29.3%) and PS 2 is 6 (1.5%). Summing these gives 115+6 = 121 patients, and 29.3%+1.5% = 30.8%.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: The 'PATIENTS' section states that 397 patients were randomized to the ADT plus Docetaxel group (Table 1). It also mentions that '6 patients in the combination group did not start the assigned therapy'. Therefore, 397 - 6 = 391 patients received docetaxel. The percentage is (391/397) * 100 = 98.5%.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: The 'PATIENTS' section states that 393 patients were randomized to the ADT Alone group (Table 1). The 'ASSIGNED THERAPY ADMINISTERED AND SUBSEQUENT THERAPY' section states that '137 patients of these patients had received docetaxel for castration-resistant prostate cancer, and another 10 patients had received docetaxel before castration resistance was confirmed'. Therefore, 137 + 10 = 147 patients in the control arm received docetaxel. The percentage is (147/393) * 100 = 37.4%.

Primary Endpoint(s).value: The primary objective was to test the hypothesis that the median overall survival would be 33.3% longer among patients receiving docetaxel added to ADT early during therapy than among patients receiving ADT alone.
Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: The value is explicitly stated in the 'METHODS' section under the paragraph starting with 'We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer...'. It is also reiterated in the 'METHODS' -> 'STUDY OVERSIGHT' section: 'The primary objective of the E3805 study was to determine whether docetaxel therapy at the beginning of ADT for metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer would result in longer overall survival than that with ADT alone.'

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous.evidence: The 'METHODS' section states, 'We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel... or ADT alone.' This indicates all patients had metastases at the time of enrollment, which aligns with the definition of synchronous metastases. Table 1 shows N=397 for the ADT plus Docetaxel (Treatment) arm. Therefore, 397 (100%) patients in the treatment arm had synchronous metastases.

The 'METHODS' section states, 'We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel... or ADT alone.' This indicates all patients had metastases at the time of enrollment, which aligns with the definition of synchronous metastases. Table 1 shows N=393 for the ADT Alone (Control) arm. Therefore, 393 (100%) patients in the control arm had synchronous metastases.

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronou.evidence: The study enrolled patients who already had metastatic disease at the time of randomization. There is no information provided on patients who developed metachronous metastases (i.e., after diagnosis of prostate cancer but before enrollment or during the study as a separate category for the treatment arm).

The study enrolled patients who already had metastatic disease at the time of randomization. There is no information provided on patients who developed metachronous metastases (i.e., after diagnosis of prostate cancer but before enrollment or during the study as a separate category for the control arm).

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: The proportion of patients who had a decrease in the PSA level to less than 0.2 ng per milliliter at 6 months and 12 months; the median time to the development of castration-resistant prostate cancer (biochemical, symptomatic, or radiographic); and the median time to clinical progression.
Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: The 'SECONDARY END POINTS AND TOXIC EFFECTS' section on page 742 explicitly lists these as secondary endpoints. Table 2, titled 'Secondary End Points', also details these outcomes.

Gleason score - N (%) | ≤ 7 | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 1, under 'Gleason score—no. (%)' for 'ADT plus Docetaxel (N=397)'. Summed the counts and percentages for '4-6' (21 (5.3%)) and '7' (96 (24.2%)) to get 117 (29.5%).

Gleason score - N (%) | ≤ 7 | Control.evidence: Found in Table 1, under 'Gleason score—no. (%)' for 'ADT Alone (N=393)'. Summed the counts and percentages for '4-6' (21 (5.3%)) and '7' (83 (21.1%)) to get 104 (26.4%).

Gleason score - N (%) | ≥ 8 | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 1, under 'Gleason score—no. (%)' for 'ADT plus Docetaxel (N=397)' in the row '8–10'.

Gleason score - N (%) | ≥ 8 | Control.evidence: Found in Table 1, under 'Gleason score—no. (%)' for 'ADT Alone (N=393)' in the row '8–10'.

TTPSA (mo) | Treatment.evidence: In the 'RESULTS' section, under 'SECONDARY END POINTS AND TOXIC EFFECTS', it states: 'The median time to the development of castration-resistant prostate cancer (biochemical, symptomatic, or radiographic) was 20.2 months with combination therapy'. Table 2 also confirms this value for 'ADT plus Docetaxel'.

TTPSA (mo) | Control.evidence: In the 'RESULTS' section, under 'SECONDARY END POINTS AND TOXIC EFFECTS', it states: 'The median time to the development of castration-resistant prostate cancer (biochemical, symptomatic, or radiographic) was ... as compared with 11.7 months with ADT alone'. Table 2 also confirms this value for 'ADT Alone'.

Type of Therapy.value: Androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) alone or concomitant treatment with ADT plus docetaxel (at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area every 3 weeks for six cycles).
Type of Therapy.evidence: The ABSTRACT section states: 'We assessed whether concomitant treatment with ADT plus docetaxel would result in longer overall survival than that with ADT alone.' The METHODS section further elaborates: 'We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel (at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area every 3 weeks for six cycles) or ADT alone.'

Treatment Class.evidence: The title of the article is "Chemohormonal Therapy in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer". The study investigates the combination of androgen-deprivation therapy (hormonal therapy) and docetaxel (chemotherapy), which falls under this classification.

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: The median overall survival for ADT plus docetaxel (combination therapy) was reported as 57.6 months in the ABSTRACT section (ID: 57d4780d-4753-4201-a98a-c145e7f03443) and confirmed in the RESULTS section (ID: 60353ba2-9c97-4cf0-938e-a5401feb3360) and Figure 1A.

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Control.evidence: The median overall survival for ADT alone was reported as 44.0 months in the ABSTRACT section (ID: 57d4780d-4753-4201-a98a-c145e7f03443) and confirmed in the RESULTS section (ID: 60353ba2-9c97-4cf0-938e-a5401feb3360) and Figure 1A.

Median OS (mo) | High volume | Treatment.evidence: In the RESULTS section (ID: 60353ba2-9c97-4cf0-938e-a5401feb3360), it is stated that for the subgroup with high-volume disease, the median overall survival in the combination group was 49.2 months. This is also shown in Figure 1B.

Median OS (mo) | High volume | Control.evidence: In the RESULTS section (ID: 60353ba2-9c97-4cf0-938e-a5401feb3360), it is stated that for the subgroup with high-volume disease, the median overall survival in the ADT-alone group was 32.2 months. This is also shown in Figure 1B.

Median OS (mo) | Low volume | Treatment.evidence: The RESULTS section (ID: 60353ba2-9c97-4cf0-938e-a5401feb3360) states, 'The median survival at the time of the analysis had not been reached in the subgroup with low-volume disease in either study group (Fig. 1C).' Figure 1C also indicates 'NR' (Not Reached) for ADT+docetaxel.

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.evidence: The document provides a table of Grade 3 or higher adverse events for the treatment arm (Table 3), but the context and footnotes indicate these events were captured to ascertain the adverse-event profile of chemotherapy and had an attribution of relatedness to study therapy. A distinct 'all-cause' total for grade 3 or higher events, explicitly stated as such, is not provided.

The 'MONITORING OF TOXIC EFFECTS AND EFFICACY' section states: 'Adverse events among patients assigned to ADT alone were not routinely documented, although major adverse events were to be reported.' No specific numbers or percentages for all-cause grade 3 or higher events are provided for the control arm.

Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.evidence: Found in Table 3, row 'Any event', columns 'Grade 3', 'Grade 4', and 'Grade 5' for the 'ADT plus Docetaxel' group. The total number of patients in this group with follow-up data was 390. Grade 3: 65 (16.7%), Grade 4: 49 (12.6%), Grade 5: 1 (0.3%). Summing these gives 115 patients, which is (65+49+1)/390 * 100 = 29.48%, rounded to 29.6%. The footnote for Table 3 states: 'All grade 3 or higher toxic effects in the combination group were captured, and an attribution of relatedness to study therapy was made by the local investigators.' This indicates these are treatment-related.

The 'MONITORING OF TOXIC EFFECTS AND EFFICACY' section states: 'Adverse events among patients assigned to ADT alone were not routinely documented, although major adverse events were to be reported.' No specific numbers or percentages for treatment-related grade 3 or higher events are provided for the control arm.

Found in Table 3, row 'Any event', column 'Grade 5' for the 'ADT plus Docetaxel' group. The total number of patients in this group with follow-up data was 390. The footnote for Table 3 states: 'All grade 3 or higher toxic effects in the combination group were captured, and an attribution of relatedness to study therapy was made by the local investigators.' This indicates these are treatment-related.

The 'MONITORING OF TOXIC EFFECTS AND EFFICACY' section states: 'Adverse events among patients assigned to ADT alone were not routinely documented, although major adverse events were to be reported.' No specific numbers or percentages for treatment-related grade 5 events are provided for the control arm.
